title: Ground Zero: Six Words, Six Hooks
genre: Intimate Croatian klapa-inspired intro, soft tamburica, mandolin, hand percussion, warm Adriatic atmosphere, spoken-sung male vocal, 74 BPM
note: Rthmix: Croatian Starter. Ground zero explains how to use the Memory Rthmix: one word, one hook, one track at a time.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
This is not a list to rush
this is six doors, one by one
one word lands, one hook holds
play the track until it comes

[CHORUS]
Hear the word
catch the hook
say the word again
one unlock, one small chain
then move to the next one

[VERSE 2]
Each Rthm gives one Croatian word
not a lesson, not a flood
the sound will point you to the meaning
the meaning brings the sound back up

[BRIDGE]
If the hook feels odd, let it be odd
sticky is better than clever
the word starts the track
the word ends the track
and the bridge sits in the centre

[OUTRO]
One word, one hook
one Rthm at a time
hvala, molim, da, ne
voda, kruh
six words in line

title: Hvala: The First Door
genre: Gentle Dalmatian klapa pop, soft male harmony, tamburica, nylon guitar, brushed percussion, warm seaside morning, 82 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter track 1: hvala = thank you. Story arc: the journey begins with gratitude.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Hvala means thank you
hvala is where we start
someone helps, the moment opens
gratitude hits the heart

[CHORUS]
Hvala sounds like voila
voila, there it is
the help appears, the thanks appears
hvala is thank you for this

[VERSE 2]
Say it at the first door: hvala
hear the little magic: voila
when something good appears for you
thank you lands as hvala

[BRIDGE]
Do not leave with only voila
voila is just the hook
the real word is hvala
write it in the book

[OUTRO]
Thank you is hvala
voila brings hvala
the word is hvala
hvala

title: Molim: The Polite Ask
genre: Croatian folk-pop groove, tamburica and accordion, soft handclaps, close male vocal, polite conversational energy, 88 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter track 2: molim = please / you're welcome. Story arc: after gratitude, the listener learns the soft ask.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Molim means please
molim can welcome you too
soft at the doorway, polite in the asking
molim is what you use

[CHORUS]
Molim sounds like moll him
but keep the feeling gentle
not a shove, not a demand
molim asks respectful

[VERSE 2]
You want the next door open
you ask without force
molim, please, molim
the polite little course

[BRIDGE]
When someone thanks you, molim can answer
you're welcome, easy and small
please and welcome share the word
molim carries them all

[OUTRO]
Please is molim
you're welcome is molim
the soft ask is molim
molim

title: Da: The Door Opens
genre: Bright Croatian tamburica pop, upbeat acoustic guitar, light kick, claps, sunny village-square chorus, male vocal, 100 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter track 3: da = yes. Story arc: the first confident yes, the album energy lifts.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Da means yes
da is short and clear
one small sound, one open door
the answer lets you near

[CHORUS]
Da is the door opening
da says yes, come through
one syllable, no heavy lifting
da is yes for you

[VERSE 2]
Hear it like a click in the lock
da, the way is free
yes is not a long word here
yes is simply da

[BRIDGE]
Do not make it complicated
short word, short path
the door opens, yes arrives
the answer is da

[OUTRO]
Yes is da
open door, da
yes is da
da

title: Ne: The Clean Boundary
genre: Percussive Balkan-Croatian folk pop, tamburica chops, frame drum, sharper vocal response, playful call and answer, 104 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter track 4: ne = no. Story arc: confidence becomes boundary.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Ne means no
ne closes the gate
one small word, one clean boundary
no delay, no debate

[CHORUS]
Ne sounds like nay
nay means no
hear the old word point the way
nay brings you to ne

[VERSE 2]
If the answer is not this time
if the door will not go
Croatian keeps it small and sharp
ne is the word for no

[BRIDGE]
Nay is only the hook
ne is what you say
no in Croatian, clean and quick
ne carries nay away

[OUTRO]
No is ne
nay points to ne
no is ne
ne

title: Voda: The River Word
genre: Flowing Croatian coastal pop, tamburica arpeggios, accordion pads, rolling percussion, sea-and-river imagery, expansive male vocal, 96 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter track 5: voda = water. Story arc: the route moves from doors into landscape and flow.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Voda means water
voda is what you drink
clear in the glass, cold in the hand
bring it to the sink

[CHORUS]
Voda starts with V
picture a V-shaped stream
water pours down both sides
voda is the scene

[VERSE 2]
The V catches the water
the water makes the word
voda, water, voda
let the pouring sound be heard

[BRIDGE]
Do not stop at the picture
the picture points one way
V-shaped water becomes voda
voda is what you say

[OUTRO]
Water is voda
V-shaped water, voda
water is voda
voda

title: Kruh: Bread at the Table
genre: Warm Croatian tavern folk ballad, tamburica, double bass, hand percussion, communal klapa harmonies, reflective closing energy, 84 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter track 6: kruh = bread. Story arc: the journey ends at the table with something shared.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Kruh means bread
kruh is on the plate
warm from the table, torn by hand
the word has weight

[CHORUS]
Kruh sounds close to crust
and crust belongs to bread
feel that edge beneath your thumb
kruh comes back instead

[VERSE 2]
Crust is just the handle
bread is what it holds
kruh, bread, kruh
the loaf comes warm and whole

[BRIDGE]
If you remember crust
you can find the bread
if you find the bread
the Croatian word is kruh

[OUTRO]
Bread is kruh
crust brings kruh
bread is kruh
kruh

title: Bonus: You Have Six
genre: Reflective Croatian klapa reprise, gentle tamburica, soft ensemble harmony, sunset coastal closing, grateful male vocal, 76 BPM
note: Rthmix Croatian Starter bonus reflection: acknowledge the six words and run the full chain once.
lyrics:
[VERSE 1]
Take the moment, count the doors
six small words are yours
not because you forced the list
because each one has a hook

[CHORUS]
Hvala, molim
da and ne
voda, kruh
they come back when you play

[VERSE 2]
Thank you, please
yes and no
water, bread
you know where they go

[BRIDGE]
Voila brings hvala
soft ask brings molim
door says da
nay says ne
V-stream brings voda
crust brings kruh again

[OUTRO]
Six words, six hooks
one chain you can use
hvala, molim, da, ne
voda, kruh
